owner.name: Fazle Rabbi Zaki
owner.title: Aerospace Engineer | GNC & ADCS
owner.affiliation: B.S.E. Aerospace Engineering, University of Michigan
owner.university: B.S.E. Aerospace Engineering, University of Michigan
owner.tagline: Controls, sensing, and hardware testing. I build, test, and verify.
about: I'm an aerospace engineer from the University of Michigan (B.S.E., May 2026, University Honors) focused on guidance, navigation, and control. I like taking a system from first-principles modeling through hardware integration to test data that closes the loop.

My flagship project was leading ADCS development on a 1U CubeSat imaging testbed. I designed an Extended Kalman Filter fusing gyroscope and magnetometer data, built a 5-state attitude controller driving a reaction wheel and magnetorquers, and developed a Python/Plotly Dash ground station streaming telemetry at 10 Hz over UDP. We demonstrated detumble from 1 rev/s to spin stoppage in under 50 seconds on a Helmholtz coil HIL testbed.

I also served as Safety, Policy, and Risk Lead on the Icarus heliophysics mission, a dual-spacecraft concept that completed NASA Phase B Preliminary Design Review. I built a 32-risk register, mapped 12 rights-holders, and ensured compliance across NPR 8715.6, ITAR/DDTC, and FCC/ITU for deep-space X/Ka-band communications.

On MASA's Limelight program - a student-built liquid bipropellant rocket flying liquid oxygen - I work on the avionics subteam: thermocouple firmware, full-stack cryogenic cold-flow test operations, E-Box sensor checkout, REDS valve debugging, and hardware-in-the-loop infrastructure for sensor and pyro telemetry verification.
I'm happiest where controls meets hardware: sensors on the bench, scripts on the laptop, and a clear test plan to verify that the math and the metal agree.

items: State estimation: TRIAD, Extended Kalman Filter
Momentum management with reaction wheels and magnetorquers
Python: NumPy, SciPy, Plotly Dash, pandas

projects.title: CubeSat ADCS Imaging Testbed
projects.summary: Designed and integrated a 1U CubeSat prototype with EKF-based attitude estimation, a 5-state mode controller, reaction wheel and magnetorquer actuators, a Helmholtz coil HIL testbed, and a real-time Python ground station. Led systems engineering for a 15-member team.
metrics: Detumbled from 1 rev/s to spin stoppage in under 50 seconds
Telemetry dashboard streamed 35-double UDP packets at 10 Hz
Final spacecraft mass: 0.95 kg against a 1U-class target
System cost: about $3,000 against a $6,000 cap

gallery.caption: Integrated 1U spacecraft bus with onboard cameras
Helmholtz coil testbed - 168 uT uniform field
Long-exposure view of the artificial starfield used for star tracker testing
Star tracker diagnostic - centroiding and pattern matching
EKF attitude estimate converging on truth
EKF angular-rate estimate vs. truth
Detumble run - state mode, wheel command, and body rate
Plotly Dash ground station streaming 10 Hz telemetry
End-to-end comms demo - validated packets with synchronized IMU and EKF data
Reaction wheel and camera mount CAD

projects.title: Icarus Heliophysics Mission
projects.role: Safety, Policy & Risk Lead; ADCS Contributor
projects.summary: Led safety, policy, and risk analysis for a dual-spacecraft heliophysics mission through NASA Phase B Preliminary Design Review. Built a 32-risk register, mapped 12 rights-holders, and ensured compliance across debris mitigation, export control, and spectrum allocation regulations.
metrics: NASA Phase B Preliminary Design Review completed
32 mission risks tracked with owner, mitigation, and status
12 rights-holders mapped on an impact-power grid
$400M FY2026 mission budget target through 2049 operations

gallery.caption: Icarus mission patch
Phase 1 - launch and gravity assist
Phase 2 - ScienceSat transit to Sun-Mars L1
Phase 3 - RelaySat transit to heliocentric orbit
Heliocentric low-thrust trajectory with burn and coast phases
ScienceSat configuration and components
RelaySat configuration and components
Power allocation and total thrust over the transfer
Mission data flow between ScienceSat, RelaySat, and Earth

projects.title: MASA Limelight Liquid Rocket
projects.summary: Avionics for Limelight, MASA's liquid bipropellant rocket (LOx oxidizer) built to launch in 2026 under FAA Advanced High Power Rocketry clearance. Thermocouple firmware, weekly full-stack cryogenic cold-flow operations, E-Box sensor checkout, REDS valve debugging, and HIL infrastructure for sensor and pyro telemetry verification.
metrics: Liquid bipropellant vehicle flying liquid oxygen with COPV pressurization
Flight Readiness Review submitted to the FAA for Advanced High Power clearance
Weekly full-stack cryogenic coldflow campaign, Jan-Apr 2026
HIL emulation of thermocouple, pressure transducer, and pyro channels

gallery.caption: Limelight mission patch - Tangerine Space Program
Night coldflow operations - oxidizer venting during a full-stack test
Vertical test stand venting during a cold-flow run
Test site during the 2026 coldflow campaign
Avionics bay board hardware
Altitude-vs-time flight telemetry with event timestamps

projects.title: Wind-Powered Generator
projects.summary: Designed and built a self-yawing 1.54 m horizontal-axis wind turbine on a $110 budget. Passed five safety inspections for rooftop deployment, instrumented performance testing, and diagnosed drivetrain losses with a recommended 1:3 gearing path.
metrics: Peak voltage: 6.45 V
Peak power: 0.17 W
Tip-speed ratio: average 2.5, maximum 7.2
Total build cost: $110

gallery.caption: Assembled turbine - hub, blades, fin, and base
Rooftop deployment at the Climate and Space Research Building
Drivetrain - stepper motor, bearing, and trapdoor nacelle
Blade frame geometry - 120 deg spacing with plastic wrap surfaces
Savonius alternative concept evaluated in the design trade
Voltage-frequency curve fit from instrumented rooftop testing

projects.title: Titan Surface Air Puck
projects.summary: Designed a hexagonal lift platform in CATIA, validated it with STAR-CCM+ CFD, and built a 269 g prototype. Achieved a 6.68 s minimum crossing time and delivered 80 g payload in competition.
metrics: CFD prediction: 1.41 N lift at 1.51 W
Final system mass: 269 g
Best crossing time: 6.68 s
Maximum payload delivered: 80 g

gallery.caption: 269 g hexagonal prototype with Arduino, motor controller, and propeller
CATIA solid model with annotated dimensions
STAR-CCM+ pressure field - 1.41 N lift at 1.51 W
Lift-vs-power shape trade study - hexagon outperforms circle, square, and triangle
Payload testing data across trials

projects.title: Hovercraft Prototype & Competition
projects.summary: Built and raced a radio-controlled skirted hovercraft under NASA-style mission constraints, with internal and external flow modeling in STAR-CCM+ and Arduino-based control. Competed across three timed courses: straight-path speed, figure-8 maneuverability, and mass retrieval.
metrics: Final system mass: 490.62 g against an 800 g limit
Total electrical power: 25 W
Straight-path course time: ~18 s
Mass retrieval: best run carried 36 payloads

gallery.caption: Annotated internal layout - motors, batteries, Arduino, and skirt
Servo-swiveled vertical motor mount for vectored thrust steering
Control electronics and wiring harness
Canvas skirt with hole-punched perimeter plate
Internal flow CFD - plenum pressure inflating the skirt
External flow CFD - drag coefficient 1.09 at 10 m/s
Milano-shaped shell design with inlet and payload placement
